title: Remove a Robot From the Robot Selection Tab
summary: Use the Robot Selection tab to remove a robot from the system by entering the robot ID or selecting the robot on screen, then clicking Remove and confirming the system reports success. The source also warns that logical removal does not guarantee the robot is no longer physically present on the floor.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a robot needs to be removed from the system through the Robot Selection tab and the operator has either the robot ID or can identify the robot directly on the screen.
not_for: Do not use this procedure as proof that the robot is no longer physically present on the floor.
Do not assume logical removal is safe if physical robot presence is uncertain.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the Robot Selection tab, identify the robot by ID or on-screen selection, remove it from the system, confirm the success indication, and verify the robot is not still physically present on the floor before treating the removal as complete.

safety_notes: The source warns that a robot can be removed logically while still remaining physically present on the floor.
If a robot is physically present after logical removal, other robots may treat the pathway as free in the system model and then encounter that robot as an obstacle.
The source states this condition should not result in a crash, but it still creates a mismatch between system state and floor state.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Robot Selection tab
Robot ID or ability to select the robot on screen
Ability to click the Remove button
Awareness of whether the robot is physically present on the floor

access_required: Access to the Robot Selection tab
system_preconditions: The robot to be removed can be identified either by robot ID or by selecting it on the screen.

visual_references.description: Robot Selection tab training frame showing add/remove workflow, including robot ID entry, robot selection, and Remove action with success confirmation.

steps.title: Open the Robot Selection tab
steps.purpose: Access the interface used to remove a robot from the system.
steps.expected_result: The Robot Selection tab is visible and ready for robot identification and removal.
steps.healthy_condition: The Robot Selection tab is displayed with the robot selection area and remove workflow controls available.
steps.failure_condition: The Robot Selection tab is not accessible or the remove workflow controls are not available.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection tab view showing the robot list or robot selection area, robot ID entry field, and Remove button.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the Robot Selection tab cannot be accessed.

source_evidence.text: Open the Robot Selection tab. [Image support needed: screen view showing the Robot Selection tab, robot list or robot selection area, robot ID entry field, and Remove button.]

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The training segment describes a Robot Selection tab where operators can add a robot by entering its robot ID and clicking Add, or remove a robot by entering the robot ID or selecting the robot and clicking Remove.
Training slide explaining how to add or remove a robot in the Robot Selection tab using a robot ID or by selecting a robot.

steps.title: Identify the robot to remove
steps.purpose: Specify the correct robot for removal using one of the source-supported selection methods.
steps.instruction: Identify the robot to remove either by entering the corresponding robot ID or by selecting the robot directly on the screen.
steps.expected_result: The intended robot is selected or identified for removal.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct robot is identified by robot ID entry or direct on-screen selection.
steps.failure_condition: The robot cannot be identified or there is uncertainty about whether the correct robot has been selected.

actions.label: Enter robot ID
actions.exact_action: Type in the corresponding robot ID.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot ID entry area and the robot selection area used to choose the robot for removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the correct robot cannot be confidently identified.

source_evidence.text: Identify the robot to remove either by entering the corresponding robot ID or by selecting the robot directly on the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: To remove a robot, you can either type in the robot ID or you can click on a robot itself and press remove.
For removal, enter the corresponding robot ID or select the robot, click Remove.

steps.title: Click Remove
steps.purpose: Execute the logical removal of the selected robot from the system.
steps.expected_result: The system processes the robot removal request.
steps.healthy_condition: The Remove action is accepted by the system for the intended robot.
steps.failure_condition: The Remove action cannot be performed or appears to target the wrong robot.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Remove button in the Robot Selection tab.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if there is uncertainty that the selected robot is the intended robot before clicking Remove.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: To remove a robot, you can either type in the robot ID or you can click on a robot itself and press remove.

steps.title: Confirm removal success
steps.purpose: Verify that the system reports the logical removal completed successfully.
steps.instruction: Check whether the system shows that the removal was successful.
steps.expected_result: The system displays a success indication for the removal.
steps.healthy_condition: The system shows that the removal was successful.
steps.failure_condition: The system does not show that the removal was successful.

actions.label: Verify success message
actions.exact_action: Check whether the system shows that the removal was successful.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System feedback indicating that removal was successful.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the system does not show that the removal was successful.

source_evidence.text: Check whether the system shows that the removal was successful.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If removal is successful, it will show the removal was successful.
The source states that successful add and remove actions generate explicit success indications in the system.

steps.title: Verify physical robot is not still on the floor
steps.purpose: Prevent a mismatch between logical system state and actual floor state after removal.
steps.instruction: Before treating the removal as complete, verify that the robot is not still physically present on the floor, because the source warns that a robot can be removed logically while still physically present.
steps.expected_result: The removed robot is not physically present on the floor, or the mismatch is recognized and escalated.
steps.healthy_condition: There is no physical robot remaining on the floor after logical removal.
steps.failure_condition: A physical robot remains on the floor after logical removal, creating a mismatch between the system and the floor state.

actions.label: Verify floor state
actions.exact_action: Verify that the robot is not still physically present on the floor.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the robot is still physically present on the floor after being removed from the system.
Stop and escalate if physical robot presence is uncertain.
Stop and escalate if logical removal creates a mismatch between the system model and the floor state.

source_evidence.text: Before treating the removal as complete, verify that the robot is not still physically present on the floor, because the source warns that a robot can be removed logically while still physically present.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You don't have a logical robot on the system, but you have a physical robot on the system.
Any robot that is about to use that pathway think that space is empty and free, it will see that robot as an obstacle... It will not crash.
Removing a robot from the system can leave a physical robot present without a logical robot entry.
A physically present but logically removed robot may be treated as an obstacle by other robots.

success_criteria: The robot is removed from the system.
The system shows that the removal was successful.
The robot is confirmed not to remain physically present on the floor.
healthy_conditions: The Robot Selection tab is accessible.
The correct robot can be identified by robot ID or on-screen selection.
The system shows that removal was successful.
No physical robot remains on the floor after logical removal.
failure_conditions: The Robot Selection tab cannot be accessed.
The correct robot cannot be confidently identified.
The system does not show that removal was successful.
The robot remains physically present on the floor after logical removal.
Physical robot presence is uncertain after logical removal.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the robot is still physically present on the floor after being removed from the system.
Escalate if physical robot presence cannot be verified.
Escalate if the system does not show that the removal was successful.
Escalate if there is a mismatch between logical system state and physical floor state.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection tab workflow for removing a robot by ID or by selecting the robot and clicking Remove, plus success confirmation behavior.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: To remove a robot, you can either type in the robot ID or you can click on a robot itself and press remove... if removal is successful, it will show the removal was successful.

You don't have a logical robot on the system, but you have a physical robot on the system... any robot that is about to use that pathway think that space is empty and free, it will see that robot as an obstacle... It will not crash.

missing_details: The source does not specify exact navigation steps to reach the Robot Selection tab.
The source does not specify the exact text or location of the success message beyond stating that removal success is shown.
The source does not define a formal escalation contact or workflow.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required.
